title: prumnopitys ferruginea
intro: Prumnopitys ferruginea, also known as the red pine or iron tree, is a species of coniferous tree native to New Zealand and Australia. It has been used in traditional medicine for its antiseptic, anti-inflammatory, and antimicrobial properties, which are said to aid in wound healing, reducing fever, and fighting off infections. The plant's extracts have been found to possess health benefits such as reducing oxidative stress, improving cardiovascular health, and exhibiting potential anti-cancer properties. Herbal preparations of Prumnopitys ferruginea are typically made from its resin, which can be distilled or infused in oil, and are often used in aromatherapy, topical creams, and supplements to harness its medicinal properties.
